283	\begin{lemma}[Existence and uniqueness of the Hilbert polynomial of a rational
284	  graded series]
285	  \label{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}
286	  \lean{Polynomial.existsUnique_hilbertPoly}
287	  \mathlibok
288	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
289	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Polynomial.HilbertPoly}).}
290	  Let \(F\) be a field of characteristic zero, let \(p \in F[X]\), and let
291	  \(d \in \mathbb{N}\). Then there is a unique polynomial \(h \in F[X]\) such that
292	  for some \(N \in \mathbb{N}\) and all \(n > N\) the coefficient of \(X^n\) in the
293	  power-series expansion of \(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d} \in F\llbracket X\rrbracket\)
294	  equals \(h(n)\):
295	  \[
296	    \exists!\, h \in F[X],\ \exists N,\ \forall n > N : \;
297	    \bigl[ X^n \bigr]\bigl( p \cdot (1-X)^{-d} \bigr) \;=\; h(n).
298	  \]
299	  Equivalently, the eventual coefficient function of any rational power series
300	  with denominator a power of \(1 - X\) is, for \(n \gg 0\), given by a unique
301	  polynomial \(h\) (Mathlib's \(\mathrm{hilbertPoly}\,p\,d\)). The hypothesis
302	  \([\mathrm{CharZero}\,F]\) is satisfied by \(F = \mathbb{Q}\), which is the
303	  instance used to obtain \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\).
304	\end{lemma}
305	
306	\begin{lemma}[Additivity of \(\dim_\kappa\) on a short exact sequence
307	  (rank--nullity)]
308	  \label{lem:finrank_ses_additive_mathlib}
309	  \lean{Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank}
310	  \mathlibok
311	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
312	  (\texttt{Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.RankNullity}).}
313	  Let \(\kappa\) be a field, \(V\) a finite-dimensional \(\kappa\)-vector space and
314	  \(W \subseteq V\) a subspace. Then
315	  \[
316	    \dim_\kappa (V / W) + \dim_\kappa W \;=\; \dim_\kappa V .
317	  \]
318	  Equivalently, \(\dim_\kappa = \operatorname{finrank}_\kappa\) is additive on a
319	  short exact sequence \(0 \to W \to V \to V/W \to 0\) of finite-dimensional
320	  \(\kappa\)-vector spaces. This is the additivity that converts the degreewise
321	  short exact sequences of graded components into additive relations among the
322	  coefficients of the Hilbert series.
323	\end{lemma}
324	
325	\begin{lemma}[The rational power series \((1 - X)^{-d}\)]
326	  \label{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}
327	  \lean{PowerSeries.invOneSubPow}
328	  \mathlibok
329	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
330	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PowerSeries.WellKnown}).}
331	  Let \(S\) be a commutative ring and \(d \in \mathbb{N}\). Then \((1 - X)^d\) is a
332	  unit in the power series ring \(S\llbracket X\rrbracket\), and Mathlib names its
333	  inverse
334	  \[
335	    \operatorname{invOneSubPow}(S, d) \;=\; (1 - X)^{-d}
336	      \;\in\; S\llbracket X\rrbracket^{\times}.
337	  \]
338	  Together with the coercion
339	  \(\operatorname{toPowerSeries} : F[X] \to F\llbracket X\rrbracket\) of a
340	  polynomial to its power series, this is the data in which a rational Hilbert
341	  series \(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d}\) is expressed; it is the same rational-series shape
342	  whose eventual coefficients are extracted by
343	  \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}.
344	\end{lemma}
345	
346	\begin{lemma}\leanok
347	[Graded Hilbert--Serre: rationality of the Hilbert series]
348	  \label{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational}
349	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational}
350	  \uses{lem:finrank_ses_additive_mathlib, lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib,
351	    def:ratHilb, lem:ratHilb_ofEventuallyZero, lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq,
352	    lem:ratHilb_bump, def:graded_subquotientHilb,
353	    lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb}
354	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Commutative Algebra", \S "Noetherian graded
355	  % rings", Proposition \texttt{proposition-graded-hilbert-polynomial} (Tag
356	  % 00K1) (read from references/hilbert-serre-algebra.tex, L13893-L13905).
357	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
358	  % "Suppose that $S$ is a Noetherian graded ring
359	  %  and $M$ a finite graded $S$-module. Consider the
360	  %  function
361	  %  $$
362	  %  \mathbf{Z} \longrightarrow K'_0(S_0), \quad
363	  %  n \longmapsto [M_n]
364	  %  $$
365	  %  see Lemma \ref{lemma-graded-module-fg}.
366	  %  If $S_{+}$ is generated by elements of degree $1$,
367	  %  then this function is a numerical polynomial."
368	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Commutative Algebra", Tag 00K1
369	  (proposition on graded Hilbert polynomials); cf.\ [Nitsure], \S 1 (Snapper's
370	  Lemma), [Hartshorne], I.7.}
371	  Let \(R\) be a Noetherian graded ring whose degree-zero part is a field
372	  \(\kappa\) and which is generated as a \(\kappa\)-algebra by finitely many
373	  elements, and let \(M\) be a finitely generated graded \(R\)-module with
374	  finite-dimensional graded components \(M_n\). Then there exist
375	  \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) and \(d \in \mathbb{N}\) and an \(N \in \mathbb{N}\)
376	  such that for all \(n > N\)
377	  \[
378	    \dim_{\kappa} M_n \;=\; \bigl[ X^n \bigr]\bigl( p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d}\bigr);
379	  \]
380	  that is, the Hilbert series \(\sum_{n \ge 0} (\dim_\kappa M_n)\, X^n\) is, up to
381	  a polynomial correction in low degrees, the rational function
382	  \(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d}\), expressed in the power-series form
383	  \(\operatorname{toPowerSeries}(p) \cdot \operatorname{invOneSubPow}(\mathbb{Q}, d)\)
384	  (\cref{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}).
385	
386	  \emph{Status.} This is the substantive half of the graded Hilbert--Serre
387	  theorem (rationality of the Hilbert series of a finitely generated graded
388	  module). It is not covered by Mathlib: \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}
389	  supplies only the converse polynomial-extraction step from a rational series, not
390	  the production of the rational series from the module. The argument is realised by
391	  an \emph{ambient subquotient induction} (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb}):
392	  the whole induction ranges over pairs of homogeneous submodules \(N' \le N\) of the
393	  single fixed graded module \(M\), and the Hilbert function is the ambient difference
394	  \(n \mapsto \dim_\kappa(N \cap M_n) - \dim_\kappa(N' \cap M_n)\). The class of such
395	  pairs is closed under the kernel and cokernel of multiplication by a degree-one
396	  element (each presented again as an ambient subquotient pair), so the inductive step
397	  never forms a graded quotient ring \(R/(x)\), a graded quotient module \(M/xM\), or a
398	  regrading of a derived carrier. The genuine Mathlib gap is thereby \emph{avoided}
399	  rather than filled; the residual obligation is the short ambient bookkeeping of the
400	  subquotient blocks (\cref{def:graded_subquotientHilb},
401	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker},
402	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff},
403	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer},
404	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb}), each of which manipulates only ambient
405	  families \(n \mapsto N_{\mathrm{aux}} \cap M_n\) of submodules of \(M\).
406	  % NOTE: The power-series rationality toolkit (\cref{subsec:isRatHilb}) is formalized
407	  %   and axiom-clean. The present theorem is not yet a Lean declaration; the ambient
408	  %   subquotient route (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb}) sidesteps missing
409	  %   graded-quotient API by working with ambient families of submodules of \(M\).
410	\end{lemma}
411	
412	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: [Stacks Project], Tag 00K1, proof, inductive step (read
413	% from references/hilbert-serre-algebra.tex, L13938-L13947).
414	% "Let $\overline{M} = M/xM$. Note that the map $x : M \to M$
415	%  is {\it not} a map of graded $S$-modules, since it does
416	%  not map $M_d$ into $M_d$. Namely, for each $d$ we have the
417	%  following short exact sequence
418	%  $$
419	%  0 \to M_d \xrightarrow{x} M_{d + 1} \to \overline{M}_{d + 1} \to 0
420	%  $$
421	%  This proves that $[M_{d + 1}] - [M_d] = [\overline{M}_{d + 1}]$.
422	%  Hence we win by Lemma \ref{lemma-numerical-polynomial}."
423	\begin{proof}
424	  \uses{def:ratHilb, lem:ratHilb_ofEventuallyZero, lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq,
425	    lem:ratHilb_bump, lem:graded_degreewise_finrank_diff,
426	    def:graded_subquotientHilb, lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker,
427	    lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff,
428	    lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer, lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb,
429	    lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep,
430	    lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq, lem:ideal_homogeneous_span_mathlib,
431	    lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib,
432	    lem:fg_restrictScalars_of_surjective_mathlib}
433	  We argue with the abstract graded \(\kappa\)-algebra
434	  \(R = \bigoplus_{n \ge 0} R_n\), whose degree-zero part \(R_0 = \kappa\) is a
435	  field, and the finitely generated graded \(R\)-module
436	  \(M = \bigoplus_{n \ge 0} M_n\) with finite-dimensional components \(M_n\);
437	  there is no appeal to any geometric realisation of \(R\) or \(M\).
438	
439	  \emph{Reduction to generation in degree \(1\).} Since \(R\) is Noetherian and
440	  finitely generated as a \(\kappa\)-algebra, it is generated by finitely many
441	  homogeneous elements of positive degree. Replacing \(R\) by a Veronese
442	  subalgebra \(R^{(e)} = \bigoplus_n R_{en}\) for a suitable \(e\) and re-grading
443	  (and \(M\) by the corresponding Veronese module), we may assume \(R\) is
444	  generated by its degree-one part, \(\kappa[R_1] = R\). The degree-one part
445	  \(R_1\) is then a finite-dimensional \(\kappa\)-vector space whose \(\kappa\)-basis
446	  \(x_1, \dots, x_r\) generates \(R\) as a \(\kappa\)-algebra; \(r = \dim_\kappa R_1\)
447	  is the number on which we induct.
448	  % NOTE: The existence of finitely many degree-one generators (the number r the
449	  % induction is on) is expected to follow from the standing hypotheses --
450	  % "finitely generated κ-algebra" together with "generated in degree 1" -- so no
451	  % new hypothesis on gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational is anticipated. To be
452	  % confirmed when the prover formalizes the assembly: degree-1 generation gives
453	  % R₁ as a finite-dimensional κ-vector space spanning R as a κ-algebra, and a
454	  % κ-basis of R₁ is a finite degree-one generating set.
455	
456	  \emph{Set-up of the ambient data.} Regard \(M\) as the fixed ambient graded
457	  \(\kappa\)-module with internal decomposition \(n \mapsto \mathcal{M}_n := M_n\)
458	  (\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}); the components are finite-dimensional. For each
459	  basis vector \(x_i \in R_1\) let \(t_i : M \to M\) be multiplication by \(x_i\), a
460	  \(\kappa\)-linear endomorphism that raises degree by one (it sends \(M_n\) into
461	  \(M_{n+1}\), \cref{lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}). Since \(R\) is
462	  commutative the \(t_i\) pairwise commute, and they trivially preserve the pair of
463	  homogeneous submodules \((N, N') = (\top, \bot)\). Because \(R\) is generated by
464	  \(x_1, \dots, x_r\) and \(M\) is finitely generated over \(R\), \(M\) is finitely
465	  generated as a module over the polynomial ring \(\kappa[x_1, \dots, x_r]\) acting
466	  through the \(t_i\); this is the finiteness condition required below.
467	
468	  \emph{Invoking the ambient subquotient induction.} Apply
469	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb} to the pair \((N, N') = (\top, \bot)\)
470	  with the \(r\) commuting degree-\((+1)\) endomorphisms \(t_1, \dots, t_r\). It
471	  yields \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}\) of order \(r\) for the ambient subquotient Hilbert
472	  function of this pair (\cref{def:graded_subquotientHilb}),
473	  \[
474	    h(n) \;=\; \dim_\kappa(\top \cap \mathcal{M}_n) - \dim_\kappa(\bot \cap \mathcal{M}_n)
475	      \;=\; \dim_\kappa M_n - 0 \;=\; \dim_\kappa M_n .
476	  \]
477	  Hence \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(n \mapsto \dim_\kappa M_n,\, r)\) (\cref{def:ratHilb}):
478	  there are \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\), \(d := r \in \mathbb{N}\) and \(N \in \mathbb{N}\)
479	  with
480	  \[
481	    \dim_\kappa M_n \;=\; \bigl[X^n\bigr]\bigl(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d}\bigr)
482	    \qquad (n > N),
483	  \]
484	  which is the claim, with pole order \(d = r\). The internal mechanics of
485	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb} -- the base case via
486	  \cref{lem:ratHilb_ofEventuallyZero}, and the inductive step assembling the
487	  kernel/cokernel closure (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker}), the degreewise
488	  difference identity (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff}, built on
489	  \(D_5\), \cref{lem:graded_degreewise_finrank_diff}) and the finiteness transfer
490	  (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer}) into
491	  \cref{lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq} and \cref{lem:ratHilb_bump} -- are the Stacks~00K1
492	  induction realised entirely with ambient homogeneous submodules of \(M\).
493	\end{proof}
494	
495	\subsection{The rationality toolkit \texorpdfstring{\(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}\)}{IsRatHilb}}
496	\label{subsec:isRatHilb}
497	% NOTE: Declarations in this subsection are currently \texttt{private} in the Lean
498	% source; \lean{} pins may not resolve until they are moved to a dedicated module.
499	
500	The proof of \cref{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational} runs the Stacks~00K1 induction
501	on the number of degree-one generators. Each inductive step manipulates the
502	\emph{shape} of the Hilbert series -- a numerator polynomial over a power of
503	\((1-X)\) -- rather than the module itself. We isolate that bookkeeping in a
504	single predicate \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}\) on functions \(f : \mathbb{N} \to
505	\mathbb{Q}\), together with the closure lemmas the induction consumes. These are
506	project-internal helpers realising standard steps of the Stacks~00K1 argument at
507	the level of power series; Mathlib supplies only the converse extraction
508	(\cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}).
509	
510	\begin{lemma}\leanok
511	[Coefficients of \((1-X)^{-1}\) times a series are partial sums]
512	  \label{lem:coeff_invOneSubPow_one_mul}
513	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coeff_invOneSubPow_one_mul}
514	  \uses{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}
515	  Let \(F \in \mathbb{Q}\llbracket X\rrbracket\) be a power series. Then for every
516	  \(n \in \mathbb{N}\) the \(n\)-th coefficient of
517	  \(\operatorname{invOneSubPow}(\mathbb{Q}, 1)\cdot F = (1-X)^{-1}\cdot F\)
518	  (\cref{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}) is the partial sum of the coefficients of
519	  \(F\):
520	  \[
521	    \bigl[X^n\bigr]\bigl((1-X)^{-1}\cdot F\bigr)
522	    \;=\; \sum_{k=0}^{n} \bigl[X^k\bigr] F .
523	  \]
524	  Equivalently, multiplication by \((1-X)^{-1} = \sum_{m\ge 0} X^m\) is the
525	  summation operator on coefficient sequences. Proved directly in Lean from the
526	  identity \(\operatorname{invOneSubPow}(\mathbb{Q},1) = \sum_{m\ge 0} X^m\) and
527	  the Cauchy product.
528	\end{lemma}
529	
530	\begin{lemma}\leanok
531	[Antidifference of a rational Hilbert series]
532	  \label{lem:rationalHilbert_antidiff}
533	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rationalHilbert_antidiff}
534	  \uses{lem:coeff_invOneSubPow_one_mul, lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}
535	  Let \(H, \delta : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{Q}\), let \(q \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) and
536	  \(e, N \in \mathbb{N}\). Suppose that for all \(n > N\) the sequence \(\delta\)
537	  is the coefficient sequence of the rational series \(q\cdot(1-X)^{-e}\),
538	  \[
539	    \delta(n) \;=\; \bigl[X^n\bigr]\bigl(q\cdot(1-X)^{-e}\bigr),
540	  \]
541	  and that \(\delta\) is the first difference of \(H\) beyond \(N\), i.e.
542	  \(H(n+1) - H(n) = \delta(n+1)\) for all \(n > N\). Then there is a numerator
543	  polynomial \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) such that for all \(n > N\)
544	  \[
545	    H(n) \;=\; \bigl[X^n\bigr]\bigl(p\cdot(1-X)^{-(e+1)}\bigr).
546	  \]
547	  That is, summing a coefficient sequence (anti-differencing) raises the pole
548	  order by one: the partial-sum series of \(q\cdot(1-X)^{-e}\) equals
549	  \(p\cdot(1-X)^{-(e+1)}\) up to a polynomial correction in low degrees. This is
550	  the power-series heart of the Stacks~00K1 inductive step.
551	\end{lemma}
552	
553	\begin{proof}
554	  \uses{lem:coeff_invOneSubPow_one_mul, lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}
555	  Write \(F = q\cdot(1-X)^{-e}\). Multiplying by
556	  \((1-X)^{-1}\) and using the factorisation
557	  \((1-X)^{-(e+1)} = (1-X)^{-1}\cdot(1-X)^{-e}\)
558	  (\cref{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}), \cref{lem:coeff_invOneSubPow_one_mul} gives
559	  \(\bigl[X^m\bigr]\bigl(q\cdot(1-X)^{-(e+1)}\bigr) = \sum_{k=0}^{m}[X^k]F\), the
560	  partial sums of \(F\). On the other hand, telescoping the first-difference
561	  hypothesis from \(N+1\) expresses \(H(N+1+j)\) as \(H(N+1)\) plus a window sum
562	  of the coefficients of \(F\). Splitting the partial sum
563	  \(\sum_{k=0}^{n}[X^k]F\) at \(N+2\) and absorbing the resulting constant
564	  discrepancy into a numerator \(C\,(1-X)^{e}\) (a constant function is the
565	  order-\((e+1)\) coefficient sequence of \(C\,(1-X)^{e}\cdot(1-X)^{-(e+1)} =
566	  C\,(1-X)^{-1}\)), the explicit numerator
567	  \(p = C\,(1-X)^{e} + q\) realises \(H(n)\) as
568	  \([X^n]\bigl(p\cdot(1-X)^{-(e+1)}\bigr)\) for all \(n > N\).
569	\end{proof}
570	
571	\begin{definition}\leanok
572	[Rational Hilbert function]
573	  \label{def:ratHilb}
574	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb}
575	  \uses{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}
576	  \textit{Source: Stacks 00K1.}
577	  For a function \(f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{Q}\) and \(d \in \mathbb{N}\), say
578	  that \(f\) is a \emph{rational Hilbert function of order \(d\)}, written
579	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(f, d)\), when there exist a numerator polynomial
580	  \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) and an \(N \in \mathbb{N}\) such that for all \(n > N\)
581	  \[
582	    f(n) \;=\; \bigl[X^n\bigr]\bigl(p\cdot(1-X)^{-d}\bigr)
583	    \;=\; \bigl[X^n\bigr]\bigl(\operatorname{toPowerSeries}(p)\cdot
584	      \operatorname{invOneSubPow}(\mathbb{Q}, d)\bigr)
585	  \]
586	  (\cref{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}). This is the ``eventually equal to the
587	  coefficient sequence of a rational series \(p\cdot(1-X)^{-d}\)'' predicate that
588	  the graded Hilbert--Serre induction tracks; its conclusion shape is exactly the
589	  one consumed by \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}.
590	\end{definition}
591	
592	\begin{lemma}\leanok
593	[Base case: eventually-zero functions are rational of order \(0\)]
594	  \label{lem:ratHilb_ofEventuallyZero}
595	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb.ofEventuallyZero}
596	  \uses{def:ratHilb}
597	  If \(f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{Q}\) is eventually zero -- there is
598	  \(N\) with \(f(n) = 0\) for all \(n > N\) -- then \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(f, 0)\)
599	  (\cref{def:ratHilb}). Indeed \(f\) is then the coefficient sequence of the
600	  numerator polynomial \(0\) over \((1-X)^{0} = 1\). This is the base case of the
601	  Stacks~00K1 induction (no degree-one generators). Proved directly in Lean.
602	\end{lemma}
603	
604	\begin{lemma}\leanok
605	[Raising the pole order]
606	  \label{lem:ratHilb_bump}
607	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb.bump}
608	  \uses{def:ratHilb, lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}
609	  If \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(f, d)\) (\cref{def:ratHilb}) then
610	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(f, d+1)\): multiplying the numerator by \((1-X)\) cancels
611	  one factor of \((1-X)^{-1}\), so the same coefficient sequence is realised at
612	  pole order \(d+1\). This lets two rational Hilbert functions of different
613	  orders be brought to a common denominator. Proved directly in Lean using
614	  \((1-X)\cdot(1-X)^{-(d+1)} = (1-X)^{-d}\) (\cref{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}).
615	\end{lemma}
616	
617	\begin{lemma}\leanok
618	[Closure under difference]
619	  \label{lem:ratHilb_sub}
620	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb.sub}
621	  \uses{def:ratHilb}
622	  If \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(f, d)\) and \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(g, d)\)
623	  (\cref{def:ratHilb}) at the same order \(d\), then
624	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(n \mapsto f(n) - g(n),\, d)\): the numerator of the
625	  difference is the difference of the numerators (over the common denominator
626	  \((1-X)^{-d}\)). Proved directly in Lean.
627	\end{lemma}
628	
629	\begin{lemma}\leanok
630	[Closure under right shift]
631	  \label{lem:ratHilb_shiftRight}
632	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb.shiftRight}
633	  \uses{def:ratHilb}
634	  If \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(f, d)\) (\cref{def:ratHilb}) then
635	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(n \mapsto f(n-1),\, d)\): the right shift on coefficient
636	  sequences is multiplication of the series by \(X\), so the numerator
637	  \(p\) is replaced by \(X\cdot p\) at the same pole order. Proved directly in
638	  Lean.
639	\end{lemma}
640	
641	\begin{lemma}\leanok
642	[Antidifference step for the predicate]
643	  \label{lem:ratHilb_antidiff}
644	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb.antidiff}
645	  \uses{def:ratHilb, lem:rationalHilbert_antidiff}
646	  Let \(H, g : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{Q}\) and \(e, N \in \mathbb{N}\). If
647	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(g, e)\) (\cref{def:ratHilb}) and \(g\) is the first
648	  difference of \(H\) beyond \(N\) (\(H(n+1) - H(n) = g(n+1)\) for all
649	  \(n > N\)), then \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(H, e+1)\). This is the predicate-level
650	  packaging of \cref{lem:rationalHilbert_antidiff}: a finite difference raises
651	  the pole order by one, the inductive heart of graded Hilbert--Serre.
652	\end{lemma}
653	
654	\begin{proof}
655	  \uses{def:ratHilb, lem:rationalHilbert_antidiff}
656	  Unfolding \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(g, e)\) supplies a numerator \(q\) and a
657	  threshold \(N_g\) with \(g(n) = [X^n](q\cdot(1-X)^{-e})\) for \(n > N_g\).
658	  Applying \cref{lem:rationalHilbert_antidiff} with this \(q\) and threshold
659	  \(\max(N, N_g)\) -- whose two hypotheses are exactly the rational form of \(g\)
660	  and the first-difference identity for \(H\) -- yields a numerator \(p\) with
661	  \(H(n) = [X^n](p\cdot(1-X)^{-(e+1)})\) for \(n > \max(N, N_g)\), i.e.
662	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(H, e+1)\).
663	\end{proof}
664	
665	\begin{lemma}\leanok
666	[Inductive-step engine from a degreewise short exact sequence]
667	  \label{lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq}
668	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb.ofDiffEq}
669	  \uses{def:ratHilb, lem:ratHilb_antidiff, lem:ratHilb_sub, lem:ratHilb_shiftRight}
670	  Let \(h_M, h_C, h_K : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{Q}\) and \(d, N \in \mathbb{N}\).
671	  Suppose \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(h_C, d)\) and \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(h_K, d)\)
672	  (\cref{def:ratHilb}), and that for all \(n > N\)
673	  \[
674	    h_M(n+1) - h_M(n) \;=\; h_C(n+1) - h_K(n).
675	  \]
676	  Then \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(h_M, d+1)\). This packages the entire power-series
677	  side of the Stacks~00K1 inductive step: \(h_M\) is the Hilbert function of
678	  \(M\), and \(h_C, h_K\) are the Hilbert functions of the cokernel
679	  \(C = M/xM\) and kernel \(K = \ker(x : M \to M(1))\) of multiplication by a
680	  degree-one element \(x\); the displayed identity is the alternating-sum
681	  consequence of the degreewise short exact sequence
682	  \(0 \to K_n \to M_n \xrightarrow{x} M_{n+1} \to C_{n+1} \to 0\). Given the
683	  inductive hypothesis that \(h_C, h_K\) are rational of order \(d\), the
684	  conclusion is rationality of \(h_M\) at order \(d+1\).
685	\end{lemma}

688	  \uses{lem:ratHilb_antidiff, lem:ratHilb_sub, lem:ratHilb_shiftRight}
689	  Set \(g(n) = h_C(n) - h_K(n-1)\). By closure under right shift
690	  (\cref{lem:ratHilb_shiftRight}) \(n \mapsto h_K(n-1)\) is rational of order
691	  \(d\), and by closure under difference (\cref{lem:ratHilb_sub}) so is \(g\). The
692	  hypothesis rewrites as \(h_M(n+1) - h_M(n) = g(n+1)\) for \(n > N\) (since
693	  \(g(n+1) = h_C(n+1) - h_K(n)\)). Applying the antidifference step
694	  (\cref{lem:ratHilb_antidiff}) to \(H = h_M\) and this \(g\) gives
695	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(h_M, d+1)\).

707	  % "Let $X\to S$ be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,

709	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
710	  %  $X$ whose schematic support is proper over $S$. Then for each $s\in S$,
711	  %  we get a polynomial ${\Phi}_s \in  \Q[\lambda]$
712	  %  which is the Hilbert polynomial of

716	  Let \(X_s\) be proper over the field \(\kappa(s)\), let \(L_s\) be an ample line
717	  bundle on \(X_s\), and let \(\mathcal{F}_s\) be a coherent sheaf on \(X_s\).
718	  Then there is a unique polynomial \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\)
719	  such that for all \(m \gg 0\)
720	  \[
721	    \Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}(m)
722	    \;=\; \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr).
723	  \]
724	  This \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) is the Hilbert polynomial of
725	  \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}.
726	\end{theorem}
727	
728	\begin{proof}
729	  \uses{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg, lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational,
730	    lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}
731	  By \cref{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg} the section graded module
732	  \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) is a finitely generated graded module over the
733	  Noetherian graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\), with finite-dimensional components and
734	  degree-zero part the field \(\kappa(s)\). Applying the abstract graded
735	  Hilbert--Serre rationality lemma (\cref{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational}) to the
736	  graded \(\kappa(s)\)-algebra \(R = R(X_s, L_s)\) and the finitely generated
737	  graded module \(M = M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) exhibits the graded Hilbert
738	  function
739	  \(m \mapsto \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\)
740	  as the eventual coefficient function of a rational series: there are
741	  \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) and \(d \in \mathbb{N}\) and an \(N\) with
742	  \(\dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
743	  = [X^m]\,(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d})\) for \(m > N\). Apply
744	  \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib} over the characteristic-zero field
745	  \(F = \mathbb{Q}\) to this pair \((p, d)\): it yields a unique polynomial
746	  \(h \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) whose values \(h(n)\) eventually equal
747	  \([X^n]\,(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d})\). Setting
748	  \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} := h\) (with the variable renamed \(\lambda\)) gives a
749	  polynomial with \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}(m) = \dim_{\kappa(s)}
750	  \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) for \(m \gg 0\). Uniqueness
751	  is the \(\exists!\) clause of \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}: any two
752	  polynomials agreeing with the Hilbert function for \(m \gg 0\) agree at
753	  infinitely many points, hence are equal.
754	\end{proof}

756	\subsection{The ambient subquotient induction for the Stacks~00K1 step}
757	\label{subsec:gradedModuleApi}
758	
759	The power-series side of \cref{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational} is fully isolated
760	in the \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}\) toolkit (\cref{subsec:isRatHilb}). What remains is
761	the \emph{graded-module} side of the Stacks~00K1 induction. Rather than build the
762	kernel and cokernel of multiplication by a degree-one element as graded modules
763	over a smaller quotient ring \(R/(x)\) -- which would force a graded structure on a
764	derived carrier -- we range the induction over \emph{pairs of homogeneous
765	submodules of a single fixed ambient graded module} \(M\). A pair \((N, N')\) with
766	\(N' \le N\) homogeneous submodules of \(M\) stands for the subquotient \(N/N'\); its
767	ambient Hilbert function is the difference \(n \mapsto \dim_\kappa(N \cap M_n) -
768	\dim_\kappa(N' \cap M_n)\). The class of such pairs, equipped with finitely many
769	pairwise-commuting degree-one endomorphisms preserving \(N\) and \(N'\), is
770	\emph{closed} under the kernel and cokernel of one of those endomorphisms, both
771	again presented as ambient subquotient pairs. Consequently no graded quotient ring,
772	graded quotient module, or regrading of a subtype/quotient carrier is ever formed:
773	every object that appears is an ambient family \(n \mapsto N_{\mathrm{aux}} \cap M_n\)
774	of submodules of the fixed \(M\).
775

776	This subsection records that machinery as a thin layer over existing Mathlib
777	scaffold: Mathlib already provides the notion of a homogeneous submodule
778	(\cref{lem:submodule_isHomogeneous_mathlib}), the homogeneity of a span of
779	homogeneous elements (\cref{lem:ideal_homogeneous_span_mathlib}), the degree-shift
780	of multiplication by a homogeneous element
781	(\cref{lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}), the rank--nullity identity
782	(\cref{lem:finrank_range_add_finrank_ker_mathlib}), and the finite-generation
783	transfer along a surjection (\cref{lem:fg_restrictScalars_of_surjective_mathlib}).
784	What is supplied here is the internal direct-sum decomposition of a homogeneous
785	submodule as an ambient family (the split \(G_1\)), the subquotient Hilbert data,
786	the kernel/cokernel closure, the degreewise difference identity, the ambient
787	finiteness transfer, and the induction itself.
788	
789	\emph{Setting for this subsection.} Let \(R = \bigoplus_{n \ge 0} R_n\) be a graded
790	ring whose degree-zero part \(R_0 = \kappa\) is a field and which is generated as a
791	\(\kappa\)-algebra in degree one, and let \(M = \bigoplus_{n \ge 0} M_n\) be a graded
792	\(\kappa\)-vector space (the fixed ambient module) with finite-dimensional
793	components \(M_n\). All submodules \(N, N', \dots\) below are homogeneous submodules
794	of \(M\), and the relevant endomorphisms are \(\kappa\)-linear maps \(M \to M\)
795	raising degree by one -- typically multiplication by a homogeneous element
796	\(x \in R_1\).

804	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib

806	  Let \(R = \bigoplus_n R_n\) be a graded ring and \(M = \bigoplus_n M_n\) an
807	  internally graded \(R\)-module. A submodule \(p \subseteq M\) is
808	  \emph{homogeneous} when it is closed under taking homogeneous components:
809	  \(m \in p\) implies each graded piece \(\mathrm{proj}_n(m) \in p\). Equivalently
810	  \(p = \bigoplus_n (p \cap M_n)\) as a set. Mathlib bundles this into a homogeneous
811	  submodule type providing order and extensionality structure; the ambient internal
812	  direct-sum decomposition \(p = \bigoplus_n (M_n \cap p)\) is supplied by the split
813	  \(G_1\) (\cref{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep},
814	  \cref{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq}).

897	[$G_1$ (independence) -- the family $n \mapsto M_n \cap p$ is independent]
898	  \label{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep}
899	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.homogeneousSubmodule_inf_iSupIndep}
900	  \uses{lem:submodule_isHomogeneous_mathlib}
901	  Let \(M = \bigoplus_n M_n\) be an internally graded \(\kappa\)-module and let
902	  \(p \subseteq M\) be a homogeneous submodule
903	  (\cref{lem:submodule_isHomogeneous_mathlib}). Then the family
904	  \(n \mapsto M_n \cap p\) of \(\kappa\)-subspaces of \(M\) is independent: for each
905	  \(n\),
906	  \[
907	    (M_n \cap p) \;\cap\; \Bigl(\bigsqcup_{m \ne n} (M_m \cap p)\Bigr) \;=\; 0 .
908	  \]
909	  The whole statement lives in the ambient module \(M\): the subspaces
910	  \(M_n \cap p\) are submodules of \(M\), never of a derived carrier \(\smash{\hat p}\).
911	\end{lemma}
912	
913	\begin{proof}
914	  \uses{lem:submodule_isHomogeneous_mathlib}
915	  The components \(M_n\) of the internal decomposition of \(M\) are independent. The
916	  subfamily \(n \mapsto M_n \cap p\) consists of submodules with \(M_n \cap p \le M_n\),
917	  so independence is inherited: a relation among the \(M_n \cap p\) is in particular a
918	  relation among the \(M_n\), forcing each term to vanish.
919	\end{proof}
920	
921	\begin{lemma}\leanok
922	[$G_1$ (supremum) -- $\bigsqcup_n (M_n \cap p) = p$ for $p$ homogeneous]
923	  \label{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq}
924	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_inf_eq}
925	  \uses{lem:submodule_isHomogeneous_mathlib}
926	  Let \(p \subseteq M\) be a homogeneous submodule
927	  (\cref{lem:submodule_isHomogeneous_mathlib}). Then
928	  \[
929	    \bigsqcup_{n} (M_n \cap p) \;=\; p .
930	  \]
931	  Together with the independence of
932	  \cref{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep}, this is the ambient internal
933	  direct sum \(p = \bigoplus_n (M_n \cap p)\), stated entirely inside \(M\) without
934	  requiring a direct-sum decomposition of the subtype \(\smash{\hat p}\).
935	\end{lemma}
936	
937	\begin{proof}
938	  \uses{lem:submodule_isHomogeneous_mathlib}
939	  The inclusion \(\bigsqcup_n (M_n \cap p) \le p\) is clear, since each
940	  \(M_n \cap p \le p\). For the reverse inclusion, take \(m \in p\) and write
941	  \(m = \sum_n \mathrm{proj}_n(m)\) with \(\mathrm{proj}_n(m) \in M_n\) using the
942	  internal decomposition of \(M\). Homogeneity of \(p\) gives
943	  \(\mathrm{proj}_n(m) \in p\), hence \(\mathrm{proj}_n(m) \in M_n \cap p\); thus
944	  \(m\) is a finite sum of elements of the \(M_n \cap p\), i.e.
945	  \(m \in \bigsqcup_n (M_n \cap p)\).
946	\end{proof}
947	
948	\subsubsection*{The ambient subquotient class and its Hilbert function}
949	
950	\begin{definition}\leanok
951	[Subquotient Hilbert data]
952	  \label{def:graded_subquotientHilb}
953	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.subquotientHilb}
954	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.SubquotientDatum}
955	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.SubquotientDatum.hilb}
956	  \uses{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep,
957	    lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq}
958	  Fix a graded \(\kappa\)-module \(\mathcal{M}\), i.e.\ \(M\) together with an
959	  internal decomposition \(M = \bigoplus_n \mathcal{M}_n\) whose components
960	  \(\mathcal{M}_n\) are finite-dimensional over \(\kappa\). A \emph{subquotient
961	  datum of length \(r\)} consists of:
962	  \begin{itemize}
963	    \item a pair of homogeneous submodules \(N' \le N \subseteq M\); and
964	    \item a family \(t : \{0, \dots, r-1\} \to (M \to_\kappa M)\) of pairwise
965	      commuting \(\kappa\)-linear endomorphisms, each raising degree by one
966	      (\(t_i(\mathcal{M}_n) \subseteq \mathcal{M}_{n+1}\)), each preserving \(N\)
967	      and \(N'\) (\(t_i N \subseteq N\), \(t_i N' \subseteq N'\)); together with
968	    \item a finiteness condition: the subquotient \(N/N'\) is a finite module over the
969	      polynomial ring \(\kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}]\) acting through the \(t_i\) (via the
970	      ambient \(t\)-stable carriers \(\mathrm{polySubmodule}\,N\),
971	      \(\mathrm{polySubmodule}\,N'\) of \cref{def:graded_polySubmodule}).
972	  \end{itemize}
973	  The pair \((N, N')\) represents the subquotient \(N/N'\). Its \emph{ambient
974	  Hilbert function} is
975	  \[
976	    \mathrm{hilb}(n) \;=\; \Bigl(\dim_\kappa (N \cap \mathcal{M}_n)
977	      \;-\; \dim_\kappa (N' \cap \mathcal{M}_n)\Bigr) \;\in\; \mathbb{Q},
978	  \]
979	  the difference of the (finite) \(\kappa\)-dimensions of the ambient intersections,
980	  computed in \(\mathbb{Z}\) and cast to \(\mathbb{Q}\). Since \(N' \le N\) one has
981	  \(N' \cap \mathcal{M}_n \le N \cap \mathcal{M}_n\), so
982	  \(\mathrm{hilb}(n) = \dim_\kappa\bigl((N \cap \mathcal{M}_n)/(N' \cap \mathcal{M}_n)\bigr)\)
983	  is the dimension of the degree-\(n\) piece of \(N/N'\). The split \(G_1\)
984	  (\cref{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep},
985	  \cref{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq}) guarantees that these ambient
986	  intersections assemble the homogeneous submodules \(N, N'\), so the data depends
987	  only on the ambient families \(n \mapsto N \cap \mathcal{M}_n\),
988	  \(n \mapsto N' \cap \mathcal{M}_n\).
989	\end{definition}
990	
991	\subsubsection*{Ambient homogeneity calculus}
992	
993	The kernel/cokernel closure and the degreewise difference identity rest on a small
994	calculus of how a degree-raising endomorphism interacts with the internal grading and
995	with the lattice of homogeneous submodules. Throughout, \(M = \bigoplus_n \mathcal{M}_n\)

1199	\subsubsection*{Closure under kernel and cokernel}
1200	
1201	The kernel/cokernel closure of the subquotient class is realised by eight ambient
1202	component facts: for a degree-raising endomorphism \(x\) and a homogeneous pair
1203	\(N' \le N\), the kernel pair is \((N \cap x^{-1}(N'),\, N')\) and the cokernel pair is
1204	\((N,\, N' + x \cdot N)\). The lemmas below record that both new pairs are homogeneous,
1205	nest correctly, are annihilated by \(x\), and are preserved by any endomorphism \(t\)
1206	commuting with \(x\) that preserves the original pair.
1207	
1208	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1209	[Homogeneity of the kernel pair's lower module]
1210	  \label{lem:graded_ker_isHomogeneous}
1211	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.ker_isHomogeneous}
1212	  \uses{lem:graded_inf_isHomogeneous, lem:graded_comap_isHomogeneous}
1213	  Let \(x\) raise degree by one (\cref{def:graded_raisesDegree}) and let \(N, N'\) be
1214	  homogeneous submodules of \(M\). Then the kernel pair's lower module
1215	  \(N \cap x^{-1}(N')\) is homogeneous. Indeed it is the intersection
1216	  (\cref{lem:graded_inf_isHomogeneous}) of the homogeneous \(N\) with the homogeneous
1217	  preimage \(x^{-1}(N')\) (\cref{lem:graded_comap_isHomogeneous}).
1218	\end{lemma}
1219	
1220	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1221	[Homogeneity of the cokernel pair's upper module]
1222	  \label{lem:graded_coker_isHomogeneous}
1223	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.coker_isHomogeneous}
1224	  \uses{lem:graded_sup_isHomogeneous, lem:graded_map_isHomogeneous}
1225	  Under the same hypotheses, the cokernel pair's upper module \(N' + x \cdot N\) is
1226	  homogeneous: it is the sum (\cref{lem:graded_sup_isHomogeneous}) of the homogeneous
1227	  \(N'\) with the homogeneous image \(x \cdot N\)
1228	  (\cref{lem:graded_map_isHomogeneous}).
1229	\end{lemma}
1230	
1231	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1232	[The kernel pair nests]
1233	  \label{lem:graded_ker_le}
1234	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.ker_le}
1235	  If \(N' \le N\) and \(x\) preserves \(N'\) (\(x \cdot N' \subseteq N'\)), then
1236	  \(N' \le N \cap x^{-1}(N')\). Indeed \(N' \le N\) by hypothesis, and \(x \cdot N'
1237	  \subseteq N'\) is exactly \(N' \subseteq x^{-1}(N')\); so \(N'\) is below the
1238	  intersection. Hence the kernel pair \((N \cap x^{-1}(N'),\, N')\) is a valid
1239	  subquotient pair (\cref{def:graded_subquotientHilb}).
1240	\end{lemma}
1241	
1242	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1243	[The cokernel pair nests]
1244	  \label{lem:graded_coker_le}
1245	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.coker_le}
1246	  If \(N' \le N\) and \(x\) preserves \(N\) (\(x \cdot N \subseteq N\)), then
1247	  \(N' + x \cdot N \le N\): both summands lie in \(N\). Hence the cokernel pair
1248	  \((N,\, N' + x \cdot N)\) is a valid subquotient pair
1249	  (\cref{def:graded_subquotientHilb}).
1250	\end{lemma}
1251	
1252	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1253	[$x$ annihilates the kernel subquotient]
1254	  \label{lem:graded_ker_annihilate}
1255	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.ker_annihilate}
1256	  The endomorphism \(x\) carries the kernel pair's lower module into \(N'\):
1257	  \(x \cdot (N \cap x^{-1}(N')) \subseteq N'\). Indeed \(N \cap x^{-1}(N') \subseteq
1258	  x^{-1}(N')\), and applying \(x\) to a preimage of \(N'\) lands in \(N'\). Thus \(x\)
1259	  acts as zero on the kernel subquotient.
1260	\end{lemma}
1261	
1262	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1263	[$x$ annihilates the cokernel subquotient]
1264	  \label{lem:graded_coker_annihilate}
1265	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.coker_annihilate}
1266	  The endomorphism \(x\) carries \(N\) into the cokernel pair's upper module:
1267	  \(x \cdot N \subseteq N' + x \cdot N\), trivially (the right summand). Thus \(x\)
1268	  acts as zero on the cokernel subquotient \(N/(N' + x \cdot N)\).
1269	\end{lemma}
1270	
1271	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1272	[Commuting endomorphisms preserve the preimage]
1273	  \label{lem:graded_comap_map_le_of_commute}
1274	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.comap_map_le_of_commute}
1275	  Let \(t\) commute with \(x\) and preserve \(N'\) (\(t \cdot N' \subseteq N'\)). Then
1276	  \(t\) preserves the preimage: \(t \cdot x^{-1}(N') \subseteq x^{-1}(N')\). Indeed for
1277	  \(m\) with \(x m \in N'\) one has \(x(t m) = t(x m) \in t \cdot N' \subseteq N'\), so
1278	  \(t m \in x^{-1}(N')\). This is what shows the remaining \(t_i\) preserve the kernel
1279	  pair's lower module \(N \cap x^{-1}(N')\).
1280	\end{lemma}
1281	
1282	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1283	[Commuting endomorphisms preserve the image]
1284	  \label{lem:graded_map_map_le_of_commute}
1285	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.map_map_le_of_commute}
1286	  Let \(t\) commute with \(x\) and preserve \(N\) (\(t \cdot N \subseteq N\)). Then
1287	  \(t\) preserves the image: \(t \cdot (x \cdot N) \subseteq x \cdot N\). Indeed for
1288	  \(m \in N\) one has \(t(x m) = x(t m)\) with \(t m \in N\), so \(t(x m) \in x \cdot
1289	  N\). This is what shows the remaining \(t_i\) preserve the cokernel pair's upper
1290	  module \(N' + x \cdot N\).
1291	\end{lemma}
1292	
1293	\begin{lemma}[Kernel/cokernel closure of the subquotient class]
1294	  \label{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker}
1295	  % This is a narrative grouping lemma: its content is realised in Lean by the eight
1296	  % ambient component facts cited in \uses{} below (no single Lean declaration),
1297	  % together with the SubquotientDatum.ker/.coker constructors that bundle them into a
1298	  % length-(r-1) datum.
1299	  \uses{lem:graded_ker_isHomogeneous, lem:graded_coker_isHomogeneous,
1300	    lem:graded_ker_le, lem:graded_coker_le, lem:graded_ker_annihilate,
1301	    lem:graded_coker_annihilate, lem:graded_comap_map_le_of_commute,
1302	    lem:graded_map_map_le_of_commute,
1303	    def:graded_subquotientHilb, lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep,
1304	    lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq, lem:ideal_homogeneous_span_mathlib,
1305	    lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib, lem:graded_comap_isHomogeneous,
1306	    lem:graded_map_isHomogeneous, lem:graded_inf_isHomogeneous,
1307	    lem:graded_sup_isHomogeneous}
1308	  Let \((N, N')\) be a subquotient datum (\cref{def:graded_subquotientHilb}) with
1309	  endomorphisms \(t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}\), and set \(x = t_{r-1}\). Form the two new
1310	  pairs of homogeneous submodules of \(M\)
1311	  \[
1312	    K \;=\; \bigl(\,N \cap x^{-1}(N'),\ N'\,\bigr),
1313	    \qquad
1314	    C \;=\; \bigl(\,N,\ N' + x \cdot N\,\bigr),
1315	  \]
1316	  where \(x^{-1}(N') = \{\,m \in M : x \cdot m \in N'\,\}\) is the preimage and
1317	  \(x \cdot N\) is the image of \(N\) under \(x\). Then:
1318	  \begin{enumerate}
1319	    \item \(N \cap x^{-1}(N')\) and \(N' + x \cdot N\) are homogeneous submodules of
1320	      \(M\), with \(N' \le N \cap x^{-1}(N')\) and \(N' + x \cdot N \le N\), so both
1321	      \(K\) and \(C\) are subquotient pairs;
1322	    \item the remaining endomorphisms \(t_0, \dots, t_{r-2}\) pairwise commute,
1323	      raise degree by one, and preserve both members of \(K\) and of \(C\); and
1324	    \item \(x\) annihilates both subquotients: \(x \cdot (N \cap x^{-1}(N')) \subseteq N'\)
1325	      and \(x \cdot N \subseteq N' + x \cdot N\).
1326	  \end{enumerate}
1327	  Consequently \(K\) and \(C\) are again subquotient data, now of length \(r-1\)
1328	  (the endomorphisms \(t_0, \dots, t_{r-2}\)), and \(K = \ker x\) and \(C =
1329	  \operatorname{coker} x\) on the subquotient \(N/N'\): \(K\) represents
1330	  \(\ker\bigl(x : N/N' \to N/N'\bigr)\) and \(C\) represents
1331	  \(\operatorname{coker}\bigl(x : N/N' \to N/N'\bigr)\). No quotient or subtype
1332	  carrier is constructed; \(K\) and \(C\) are ambient pairs of homogeneous
1333	  submodules of the fixed \(M\).
1334	\end{lemma}
1335	
1336	\begin{proof}
1337	  \uses{lem:graded_ker_isHomogeneous, lem:graded_coker_isHomogeneous,
1338	    lem:graded_ker_le, lem:graded_coker_le, lem:graded_ker_annihilate,
1339	    lem:graded_coker_annihilate, lem:graded_comap_map_le_of_commute,
1340	    lem:graded_map_map_le_of_commute,
1341	    lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep,
1342	    lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq, lem:ideal_homogeneous_span_mathlib,
1343	    lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib, lem:graded_comap_isHomogeneous,
1344	    lem:graded_map_isHomogeneous, lem:graded_inf_isHomogeneous,
1345	    lem:graded_sup_isHomogeneous}
1346	  \emph{Homogeneity.} The preimage \(x^{-1}(N')\) is homogeneous: if
1347	  \(m = \sum_n \mathrm{proj}_n(m)\) with \(x \cdot m \in N'\), then since \(x\) raises
1348	  degree by one (\cref{lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}) the components
1349	  \(x \cdot \mathrm{proj}_n(m) \in \mathcal{M}_{n+1}\) lie in distinct degrees, and
1350	  homogeneity of \(N'\) (split \(G_1\),
1351	  \cref{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq}) forces each
1352	  \(x \cdot \mathrm{proj}_n(m) \in N'\), i.e.\ \(\mathrm{proj}_n(m) \in x^{-1}(N')\).
1353	  Thus \(N \cap x^{-1}(N')\) is an intersection of homogeneous submodules, hence
1354	  homogeneous. The image \(x \cdot N\) is homogeneous with degree-\((n+1)\) piece
1355	  \(x \cdot (N \cap \mathcal{M}_n)\) by
1356	  \cref{lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}, and a sum of homogeneous
1357	  submodules is homogeneous (\cref{lem:ideal_homogeneous_span_mathlib} applied at the
1358	  module level), so \(N' + x \cdot N\) is homogeneous. The inclusions
1359	  \(N' \le N \cap x^{-1}(N')\) (as \(x \cdot N' \subseteq N'\)) and
1360	  \(N' + x \cdot N \le N\) (as \(N' \le N\) and \(x \cdot N \subseteq N\)) are
1361	  immediate. In the language of the ambient homogeneity calculus, this paragraph is
1362	  exactly: \(x^{-1}(N')\) is homogeneous (\cref{lem:graded_comap_isHomogeneous}),
1363	  \(x \cdot N\) is homogeneous (\cref{lem:graded_map_isHomogeneous}), and the
1364	  homogeneous lattice is closed under intersection
1365	  (\cref{lem:graded_inf_isHomogeneous}) and sum
1366	  (\cref{lem:graded_sup_isHomogeneous}).
1367	
1368	  \emph{Preservation.} Each \(t_i\) with \(i \le r-2\) commutes with \(x\) and
1369	  preserves \(N, N'\); hence it preserves \(x^{-1}(N')\) (if \(x t_i m = t_i x m \in
1370	  N'\) whenever \(x m \in N'\)), so it preserves \(N \cap x^{-1}(N')\), and it
1371	  preserves \(x \cdot N\) (as \(t_i x N = x t_i N \subseteq x N\)), so it preserves
1372	  \(N' + x \cdot N\). Degree-raising and commutativity are inherited.
1373	
1374	  \emph{Annihilation by \(x\).} For \(m \in N \cap x^{-1}(N')\) one has
1375	  \(x \cdot m \in N'\) by definition, so \(x\) carries the first member of \(K\) into
1376	  its second; and \(x \cdot N \subseteq N' + x \cdot N\) trivially. Thus \(x\) acts as
1377	  zero on \(N/N'\) restricted to \(K\) and on the cokernel quotient \(C\).
1378	
1379	  \emph{Identification with \(\ker x\) and \(\operatorname{coker} x\).} On the
1380	  subquotient \(N/N'\), the class of \(m \in N\) is killed by \(x\) iff
1381	  \(x \cdot m \in N'\), i.e.\ iff \(m \in N \cap x^{-1}(N')\); so \(\ker(x : N/N' \to
1382	  N/N')\) is exactly \((N \cap x^{-1}(N'))/N'\), represented by \(K\). The image of
1383	  \(x\) on \(N/N'\) is \((N' + x \cdot N)/N'\), so the cokernel
1384	  \((N/N')/\operatorname{im} x = N/(N' + x \cdot N)\) is represented by \(C\).
1385	\end{proof}
1386	
1387	\subsubsection*{The subquotient degreewise difference identity ($D_6$)}
1388	
1389	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1390	[$D_6$ -- subquotient degreewise difference]
1391	  \label{lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff}
1392	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.subquotient_degreewise_diff}
1393	  \uses{def:graded_subquotientHilb, lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker,
1394	    lem:graded_degreewise_finrank_diff, lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep,
1395	    lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq,
1396	    lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib, lem:graded_map_inf_degree_eq,
1397	    lem:graded_sup_inf_degree_eq}
1398	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Commutative Algebra", \S "Noetherian graded
1399	  % rings", Proposition \texttt{proposition-graded-hilbert-polynomial} (Tag
1400	  % 00K1), proof (read from references/hilbert-serre-algebra.tex, L13939-L13947).
1401	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 00K1 (degreewise short exact sequence).}
1402	  Let \((N, N')\) be a subquotient datum (\cref{def:graded_subquotientHilb}) with
1403	  Hilbert function \(h_M\), and let \(x = t_{r-1}\). Let \(h_K\) and \(h_C\) be the
1404	  Hilbert functions of the kernel and cokernel subquotients
1405	  \(K = (N \cap x^{-1}(N'),\, N')\) and \(C = (N,\, N' + x \cdot N)\) of
1406	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker}. Then for every \(n\)
1407	  \[
1408	    h_M(n+1) - h_M(n) \;=\; h_C(n+1) - h_K(n).
1409	  \]
1410	  Writing \(Q_n = (N \cap \mathcal{M}_n)/(N' \cap \mathcal{M}_n)\) for the
1411	  degree-\(n\) piece of \(N/N'\) (so \(\dim_\kappa Q_n = h_M(n)\)), multiplication by
1412	  \(x\) is a \(\kappa\)-linear map \(x : Q_n \to Q_{n+1}\) with
1413	  \(\ker(x : Q_n \to Q_{n+1})\) the degree-\(n\) piece of \(K\) and
1414	  \(Q_{n+1}/\operatorname{im} x\) the degree-\((n+1)\) piece of \(C\); the identity is
1415	  then the degreewise rank--nullity difference \(D_5\)
1416	  (\cref{lem:graded_degreewise_finrank_diff}). Specialising to \((N, N') = (\top,
1417	  \bot)\) recovers the classical \(0 \to \mathcal{M}_n \xrightarrow{x}
1418	  \mathcal{M}_{n+1} \to \mathcal{M}_{n+1}/x\mathcal{M}_n \to 0\).
1419	\end{lemma}
1420	
1421	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: [Stacks Project], Tag 00K1, proof, inductive step (read
1422	% from references/hilbert-serre-algebra.tex, L13939-L13947).
1423	% "Let $\overline{M} = M/xM$. Note that the map $x : M \to M$
1424	%  is {\it not} a map of graded $S$-modules, since it does
1425	%  not map $M_d$ into $M_d$. Namely, for each $d$ we have the
1426	%  following short exact sequence
1427	%  $$
1428	%  0 \to M_d \xrightarrow{x} M_{d + 1} \to \overline{M}_{d + 1} \to 0
1429	%  $$
1430	%  This proves that $[M_{d + 1}] - [M_d] = [\overline{M}_{d + 1}]$.
1431	%  Hence we win by Lemma \ref{lemma-numerical-polynomial}."
1432	\begin{proof}
1433	  \uses{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker, lem:graded_degreewise_finrank_diff,
1434	    lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq,
1435	    lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib, lem:graded_map_inf_degree_eq,
1436	    lem:graded_sup_inf_degree_eq}
1437	  Since \(N' \le N\) are homogeneous, the split \(G_1\)
1438	  (\cref{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq}) identifies the degree-\(n\) piece
1439	  of \(N/N'\) with \(Q_n = (N \cap \mathcal{M}_n)/(N' \cap \mathcal{M}_n)\), a
1440	  finite-dimensional \(\kappa\)-vector space of dimension \(h_M(n)\). Because \(x\)
1441	  raises degree by one and preserves \(N\) and \(N'\)
1442	  (\cref{lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}), it induces a \(\kappa\)-linear
1443	  map \(x : Q_n \to Q_{n+1}\).
1444	
1445	  \emph{Kernel.} A class \([m] \in Q_n\) (with \(m \in N \cap \mathcal{M}_n\)) lies in
1446	  \(\ker(x : Q_n \to Q_{n+1})\) iff \(x \cdot m \in N' \cap \mathcal{M}_{n+1}\), i.e.\
1447	  iff \(m \in (N \cap x^{-1}(N')) \cap \mathcal{M}_n\). Hence \(\ker(x : Q_n \to
1448	  Q_{n+1}) = \bigl((N \cap x^{-1}(N')) \cap \mathcal{M}_n\bigr)/(N' \cap \mathcal{M}_n)\)
1449	  is exactly the degree-\(n\) piece of \(K\), of dimension \(h_K(n)\)
1450	  (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker}).
1451	
1452	  \emph{Cokernel.} The image of \(x : Q_n \to Q_{n+1}\) is
1453	  \(\bigl(x \cdot (N \cap \mathcal{M}_n) + (N' \cap \mathcal{M}_{n+1})\bigr)/(N' \cap
1454	  \mathcal{M}_{n+1})\). Using the ambient identity \((N' + x \cdot N) \cap
1455	  \mathcal{M}_{n+1} = (N' \cap \mathcal{M}_{n+1}) + x \cdot (N \cap \mathcal{M}_n)\)
1456	  -- which is the ambient distributive law (\cref{lem:graded_sup_inf_degree_eq})
1457	  applied to \(P = N'\), \(Q = x \cdot N\), combined with the ambient image identity
1458	  \((x \cdot N) \cap \mathcal{M}_{n+1} = x \cdot (N \cap \mathcal{M}_n)\)
1459	  (\cref{lem:graded_map_inf_degree_eq}) -- the cokernel \(Q_{n+1}/\operatorname{im} x = (N \cap
1460	  \mathcal{M}_{n+1})/\bigl((N' + x \cdot N) \cap \mathcal{M}_{n+1}\bigr)\) is the
1461	  degree-\((n+1)\) piece of \(C\), of dimension \(h_C(n+1)\).
1462	
1463	  \emph{Difference.} Apply the degreewise rank--nullity difference \(D_5\)
1464	  (\cref{lem:graded_degreewise_finrank_diff}) to \(\varphi = (x : Q_n \to Q_{n+1})\),
1465	  with \(V = Q_n\), \(W = Q_{n+1}\):
1466	  \[
1467	    h_M(n+1) - h_M(n)
1468	      = \dim_\kappa Q_{n+1} - \dim_\kappa Q_n
1469	      = \dim_\kappa(Q_{n+1}/\operatorname{im}\varphi) - \dim_\kappa(\ker\varphi)
1470	      = h_C(n+1) - h_K(n).
1471	  \]
1472	  This is the \(\mathrm{hdiff}\) hypothesis consumed by \cref{lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq}.
1473	\end{proof}
1474	
1475	\subsubsection*{The free polynomial-ring module structure}
1476	
1477	The finiteness condition of a subquotient datum (\cref{def:graded_subquotientHilb}) and
1478	its transfer down one generator (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer}) are
1479	phrased over the \emph{free} polynomial ring \(\kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}] =
1480	\operatorname{MvPolynomial}(\{0, \dots, r-1\}, \kappa)\). The following blocks build the
1481	action of that ring on the ambient module \(M\) from the \(r\) pairwise-commuting
1482	degree-raising endomorphisms \(t_i\), keeping every carrier an ambient submodule of
1483	\(M\). The free polynomial ring -- rather than the subalgebra
1484	\(\mathrm{adjoin}_\kappa\{t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}\} \subseteq \operatorname{End}_\kappa(M)\)
1485	-- is used precisely so the inductive transfer has the ring surjection
1486	\(\kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}] \twoheadrightarrow \kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-2}]\) available
1487	(\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer}).
1488	
1489	\begin{definition}\leanok
1490	[Polynomial evaluation ring homomorphism]
1491	  \label{def:graded_polyEndHom}
1492	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.polyEndHom}
1493	  \uses{lem:adjoinCommRingOfComm_mathlib}
1494	  Let \(t : \{0, \dots, r-1\} \to \operatorname{End}_\kappa(M)\) be a family of
1495	  pairwise-commuting \(\kappa\)-linear endomorphisms. The \emph{polynomial evaluation
1496	  ring homomorphism}
1497	  \[
1498	    \mathrm{polyEndHom}(t) : \kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}] \longrightarrow
1499	      \operatorname{End}_\kappa(M)
1500	  \]
1501	  sends each variable \(X_i\) to \(t_i\). It is constructed by evaluating into the
1502	  \emph{commutative} \(\kappa\)-subalgebra \(A = \mathrm{adjoin}_\kappa(\{t_i\})
1503	  \subseteq \operatorname{End}_\kappa(M)\) -- commutative by
1504	  \cref{lem:adjoinCommRingOfComm_mathlib} because the generators commute -- and then
1505	  including \(A \hookrightarrow \operatorname{End}_\kappa(M)\); evaluation into the
1506	  noncommutative \(\operatorname{End}_\kappa(M)\) directly is not an algebra
1507	  homomorphism, which is why the commutative \(A\) is interposed.
1508	\end{definition}
1509	
1510	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1511	[Evaluation sends $X_i$ to $t_i$]
1512	  \label{lem:graded_polyEndHom_X}
1513	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.polyEndHom_X}
1514	  \uses{def:graded_polyEndHom}
1515	  \(\mathrm{polyEndHom}(t)(X_i) = t_i\) for each \(i\). Immediate from the construction
1516	  (\cref{def:graded_polyEndHom}), as evaluation sends \(X_i\) to the chosen image
1517	  \(t_i\).
1518	\end{lemma}
1519	
1520	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1521	[Evaluation sends a constant to a scalar]
1522	  \label{lem:graded_polyEndHom_C}
1523	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.polyEndHom_C}
1524	  \uses{def:graded_polyEndHom}
1525	  \(\mathrm{polyEndHom}(t)(C\,c) = c \cdot \mathrm{id}_M\) for each \(c \in \kappa\):
1526	  the evaluation homomorphism (\cref{def:graded_polyEndHom}) is \(\kappa\)-linear and
1527	  carries the constant polynomial \(C\,c\) to the scalar endomorphism \(c \cdot 1\).
1528	\end{lemma}
1529	
1530	\begin{definition}\leanok
1531	[Polynomial-ring module structure on $M$]
1532	  \label{def:graded_polyModule}
1533	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.polyModule}
1534	  \uses{def:graded_polyEndHom}
1535	  The \emph{polynomial-module structure} is the \(\kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}]\)-module
1536	  structure on \(M\) obtained by restricting scalars along
1537	  \(\mathrm{polyEndHom}(t)\) (\cref{def:graded_polyEndHom}): a polynomial \(p\) acts on
1538	  \(m \in M\) by \(p \cdot m = \mathrm{polyEndHom}(t)(p)(m)\). This is the module over the
1539	  free polynomial ring on which the ambient subquotient induction's finiteness condition
1540	  is measured.
1541	\end{definition}
1542	
1543	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1544	[$X_i$ acts as $t_i$]
1545	  \label{lem:graded_polyModule_X_smul}
1546	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.polyModule_X_smul}
1547	  \uses{def:graded_polyModule, lem:graded_polyEndHom_X}
1548	  In the polynomial-module structure (\cref{def:graded_polyModule}), \(X_i \cdot m =
1549	  t_i(m)\) for all \(m \in M\). Immediate from
1550	  \(\mathrm{polyEndHom}(t)(X_i) = t_i\) (\cref{lem:graded_polyEndHom_X}).
1551	\end{lemma}
1552	
1553	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1554	[A constant acts as a scalar]
1555	  \label{lem:graded_polyModule_C_smul}
1556	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.polyModule_C_smul}
1557	  \uses{def:graded_polyModule, lem:graded_polyEndHom_C}
1558	  In the polynomial-module structure (\cref{def:graded_polyModule}), \((C\,c) \cdot m =
1559	  c \cdot m\) for all \(c \in \kappa\), \(m \in M\). Immediate from
1560	  \(\mathrm{polyEndHom}(t)(C\,c) = c \cdot \mathrm{id}_M\)
1561	  (\cref{lem:graded_polyEndHom_C}).
1562	\end{lemma}
1563	
1564	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1565	[$\kappa$-scalar tower]
1566	  \label{lem:graded_polyModule_isScalarTower}
1567	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.polyModule_isScalarTower}
1568	  \uses{def:graded_polyModule, lem:graded_polyEndHom_C}
1569	  The polynomial-module structure (\cref{def:graded_polyModule}) is compatible with the
1570	  original \(\kappa\)-action: \(\mathrm{IsScalarTower}\,\kappa\,\kappa[t_0, \dots,
1571	  t_{r-1}]\,M\). Indeed the algebra map \(\kappa \to \kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}]\)
1572	  followed by the polynomial action recovers the \(\kappa\)-action, since a scalar
1573	  \(c \in \kappa\) maps to the constant \(C\,c\), which acts as \(c\)
1574	  (\cref{lem:graded_polyEndHom_C}).
1575	\end{lemma}
1576	
1577	\begin{definition}\leanok
1578	[Polynomial-ring submodule from a $t$-stable submodule]
1579	  \label{def:graded_polySubmodule}
1580	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.polySubmodule}
1581	  \uses{def:graded_polyModule, lem:graded_polyModule_X_smul,
1582	    lem:graded_polyModule_C_smul}
1583	  Let \(N \subseteq M\) be a \(\kappa\)-submodule that is stable under each \(t_i\)
1584	  (\(t_i \cdot N \subseteq N\)). Then \(N\), with the \emph{same} ambient carrier, is a
1585	  \(\kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}]\)-submodule of \(M\) for the polynomial-module structure
1586	  (\cref{def:graded_polyModule}). Stability under the action of an arbitrary polynomial
1587	  follows by induction on monomials: constants act as scalars
1588	  (\cref{lem:graded_polyModule_C_smul}) and multiplication by \(X_i\) acts as \(t_i\)
1589	  (\cref{lem:graded_polyModule_X_smul}), both of which preserve \(N\).
1590	\end{definition}
1591	
1592	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1593	[The polynomial submodule has the same carrier]
1594	  \label{lem:graded_polySubmodule_coe}
1595	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.polySubmodule_coe}
1596	  \uses{def:graded_polySubmodule}
1597	  The underlying set of \(\mathrm{polySubmodule}(t, N)\)
1598	  (\cref{def:graded_polySubmodule}) equals \(N\): the lift to a
1599	  \(\kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}]\)-submodule does not change the carrier, so every object
1600	  in the induction remains an ambient submodule of the fixed \(M\).
1601	\end{lemma}
1602	
1603	\subsubsection*{Ambient finiteness transfer}
1604	
1605	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1606	[Finiteness transfer for the subquotients]
1607	  \label{lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer}
1608	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.subquotient_finite_transfer}
1609	  \uses{def:graded_subquotientHilb, lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker,
1610	    lem:fg_restrictScalars_of_surjective_mathlib, lem:adjoinCommRingOfComm_mathlib,
1611	    def:graded_polyEndHom, def:graded_polyModule, def:graded_polySubmodule,
1612	    lem:graded_polyModule_isScalarTower}
1613	  Let \((N, N')\) be a subquotient datum of length \(r \ge 1\)
1614	  (\cref{def:graded_subquotientHilb}), so that \(M\) is finite over
1615	  \(\kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}]\), and let \(x = t_{r-1}\). Then the two
1616	  subquotients
1617	  \[
1618	    K = (N \cap x^{-1}(N'),\, N'), \qquad C = (N,\, N' + x \cdot N)
1619	  \]
1620	  of \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker} are finite modules over
1621	  \(\kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-2}]\); that is, they are subquotient data of length
1622	  \(r-1\), satisfying the finiteness condition. This is what is needed for the
1623	  inductive hypothesis of \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb}.
1624	
1625	  The kernel \(K\) and cokernel \(C\) of \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker}
1626	  satisfy the same conditions as the original datum -- a homogeneous pair, pairwise-commuting
1627	  degree-raising endomorphisms preserving the pair, a polynomial-ring module structure, and
1628	  the finiteness condition -- but of length \(r-1\) rather than \(r\).
1629	\end{lemma}
1630	
1631	\begin{proof}
1632	  \uses{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker,
1633	    lem:fg_restrictScalars_of_surjective_mathlib, lem:adjoinCommRingOfComm_mathlib,
1634	    def:graded_polyEndHom, def:graded_polyModule, def:graded_polySubmodule,
1635	    lem:graded_polyModule_isScalarTower}
1636	  \emph{The polynomial module structure on \(M\).} The \(r\) pairwise-commuting
1637	  degree-raising endomorphisms \(x_0, \dots, x_{r-1}\) generate a \(\kappa\)-subalgebra
1638	  \(A = \mathrm{adjoin}_\kappa\{x_0, \dots, x_{r-1}\}\) of the \emph{noncommutative}
1639	  endomorphism algebra \(\operatorname{End}_\kappa(M)\); because the generators
1640	  commute pairwise, \(A\) is commutative (\cref{lem:adjoinCommRingOfComm_mathlib}).
1641	  Commutativity of \(A\) is exactly what makes the evaluation homomorphism at the
1642	  generators a legal \(\kappa\)-algebra homomorphism \(\kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}]
1643	  \to A\): evaluation refuses the noncommutative \(\operatorname{End}_\kappa(M)\) as a
1644	  target. Composing with the inclusion \(A \subseteq \operatorname{End}_\kappa(M)\)
1645	  and restricting scalars along it endows \(M\) with the structure of a module over
1646	  \(\kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}]\), compatible with the \(\kappa\)-action and
1647	  characterised by \(t_i \cdot m = x_i m\).
1648	
1649	  \emph{Transfer down one generator.} Set \(A = \kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}]\) and
1650	  \(x = t_{r-1}\). Since \(M\) is finite over the Noetherian ring \(A\), \(M\) is a
1651	  Noetherian \(A\)-module, so the submodule \(N \cap x^{-1}(N') \subseteq M\) is
1652	  finite over \(A\) and the quotients \((N \cap x^{-1}(N'))/N'\) and
1653	  \(N/(N' + x \cdot N)\) are finite over \(A\). By
1654	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker} the element \(x = t_{r-1}\) annihilates
1655	  both subquotients, so the \(A\)-action on each factors through the surjection of
1656	  polynomial rings
1657	  \[
1658	    A = \kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}] \twoheadrightarrow \kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-2}]
1659	  \]
1660	  fixing \(t_i\) for \(i < r-1\) and sending \(t_{r-1} \mapsto 0\). Applying the
1661	  finite-generation transfer along a surjection
1662	  (\cref{lem:fg_restrictScalars_of_surjective_mathlib}) turns finiteness over \(A\)
1663	  into finiteness over \(\kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-2}]\), supplying the length-\((r-1)\)
1664	  finiteness witnesses for \(K\) and \(C\).
1665	
1666	  \emph{Why the free polynomial ring.} The smaller ring must be the \emph{free}
1667	  polynomial ring \(\kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-2}]\), not the subalgebra
1668	  \(\mathrm{adjoin}_\kappa\{x_0, \dots, x_{r-2}\} \subseteq
1669	  \operatorname{End}_\kappa(M)\): relations among the endomorphisms inside
1670	  \(\operatorname{End}_\kappa(M)\) would obstruct the polynomial-ring surjection that
1671	  the scalar transfer requires. Keeping free polynomial rings at every step keeps the
1672	  surjection -- and hence the transfer -- available throughout. Everything is finite
1673	  generation of ambient submodules of the fixed \(M\) and their quotients.
1674	\end{proof}

1676	\subsubsection*{The degreewise difference identity}
1677	
1678	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1679	[$D_5$ -- degreewise rank--nullity difference]
1680	  \label{lem:graded_degreewise_finrank_diff}
1681	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.degreewise_finrank_diff}
1682	  \uses{lem:finrank_range_add_finrank_ker_mathlib,
1683	    lem:finrank_ses_additive_mathlib}
1684	  Let \(\varphi : V \to W\) be a \(\kappa\)-linear map between finite-dimensional
1685	  \(\kappa\)-vector spaces. Then
1686	  \[
1687	    \dim_\kappa W - \dim_\kappa V
1688	      \;=\; \dim_\kappa(W/\operatorname{range}\varphi) - \dim_\kappa(\ker\varphi).
1689	  \]
1690	  Applied degreewise with \(\varphi = (x : \mathcal{M}_n \to \mathcal{M}_{n+1})\),
1691	  \(V = \mathcal{M}_n\), \(W = \mathcal{M}_{n+1}\) -- so that \(\ker\varphi\) is the
1692	  degree-\(n\) piece \(K_n\) of the kernel subquotient and
1693	  \(W/\operatorname{range}\varphi = \mathcal{M}_{n+1}/x \mathcal{M}_n\) the
1694	  degree-\((n+1)\) piece \(C_{n+1}\) of the cokernel subquotient
1695	  (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff}, via the split \(G_1\)
1696	  \cref{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq} and the degree shift
1697	  \cref{lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}) -- this gives, for every \(n\),
1698	  \[
1699	    \dim_\kappa \mathcal{M}_{n+1} - \dim_\kappa \mathcal{M}_n
1700	      \;=\; \dim_\kappa C_{n+1} - \dim_\kappa K_n,
1701	  \]
1702	  which is the difference identity \(\mathrm{hdiff}\) consumed by
1703	  \cref{lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq}. No graded structure is used in proving the
1704	  underlying vector-space identity -- it is pure rank--nullity.
1705	\end{lemma}
1706	
1707	\begin{proof}
1708	  \uses{lem:finrank_range_add_finrank_ker_mathlib,
1709	    lem:finrank_ses_additive_mathlib}
1710	  By rank--nullity (\cref{lem:finrank_range_add_finrank_ker_mathlib}),
1711	  \(\dim_\kappa V = \dim_\kappa(\ker\varphi) + \dim_\kappa(\operatorname{range}
1712	  \varphi)\), so \(\dim_\kappa(\operatorname{range}\varphi) = \dim_\kappa V -
1713	  \dim_\kappa(\ker\varphi)\). By additivity on the short exact sequence
1714	  \(0 \to \operatorname{range}\varphi \to W \to W/\operatorname{range}\varphi \to 0\)
1715	  (\cref{lem:finrank_ses_additive_mathlib}),
1716	  \(\dim_\kappa(W/\operatorname{range}\varphi) = \dim_\kappa W -
1717	  \dim_\kappa(\operatorname{range}\varphi) = \dim_\kappa W - \dim_\kappa V +
1718	  \dim_\kappa(\ker\varphi)\). Rearranging gives
1719	  \(\dim_\kappa W - \dim_\kappa V = \dim_\kappa(W/\operatorname{range}\varphi) -
1720	  \dim_\kappa(\ker\varphi)\). The degreewise application identifies
1721	  \(\ker(x : \mathcal{M}_n \to \mathcal{M}_{n+1})\) and
1722	  \(\mathcal{M}_{n+1}/\operatorname{range}(x : \mathcal{M}_n \to \mathcal{M}_{n+1})\)
1723	  with the degree-\(n\) piece of the kernel subquotient and the degree-\((n+1)\)
1724	  piece of the cokernel subquotient (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff}),
1725	  the latter because the degree-\((n+1)\) graded piece of the homogeneous submodule
1726	  \(x\mathcal{M}\) is exactly \(x \mathcal{M}_n\)
1727	  (\cref{lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}).
1728	\end{proof}

1101	section LastVar
1102	
1103	variable {κ : Type*} [Field κ]
1104	
1105	/-- The `κ`-algebra surjection `MvPolynomial (Fin (r+1)) κ ↠ MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ` sending the
1106	last variable `X (Fin.last r)` to `0` and `X (Fin.castSucc i)` to `X i`. Project-local: the ring
1107	surjection along which the finiteness transfer factors
1108	(`lem:fg_restrictScalars_of_surjective_mathlib`). -/
1109	noncomputable def lastVarAlgHom (r : ℕ) (κ : Type*) [Field κ] :
1110	    MvPolynomial (Fin (r + 1)) κ →ₐ[κ] MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ :=
1111	  MvPolynomial.aeval (Fin.lastCases 0 (fun i => MvPolynomial.X i))
1112	
1113	@[simp] lemma lastVarAlgHom_X_castSucc (r : ℕ) (i : Fin r) :
1114	    lastVarAlgHom r κ (MvPolynomial.X (Fin.castSucc i)) = MvPolynomial.X i := by
1115	  simp [lastVarAlgHom]
1116	
1117	@[simp] lemma lastVarAlgHom_X_last (r : ℕ) :
1118	    lastVarAlgHom r κ (MvPolynomial.X (Fin.last r)) = 0 := by
1119	  simp [lastVarAlgHom]
1120	
1121	@[simp] lemma lastVarAlgHom_C (r : ℕ) (c : κ) :
1122	    lastVarAlgHom r κ (MvPolynomial.C c) = MvPolynomial.C c := by
1123	  simp [lastVarAlgHom]
1124	
1125	/-- `lastVarAlgHom` is a left inverse of `rename Fin.castSucc`, hence surjective. -/
1126	lemma lastVarAlgHom_rename_castSucc (r : ℕ) (q : MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ) :
1127	    lastVarAlgHom r κ (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.castSucc q) = q := by
1128	  induction q using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
1129	  | C a => simp
1130	  | add p q hp hq => simp [hp, hq]
1131	  | mul_X p i hp => simp [hp]
1132	
1133	lemma lastVarAlgHom_surjective (r : ℕ) : Function.Surjective (lastVarAlgHom r κ) :=
1134	  fun q => ⟨MvPolynomial.rename Fin.castSucc q, lastVarAlgHom_rename_castSucc r q⟩
1135	
1136	instance lastVarAlgHom_ringHomSurjective (r : ℕ) :
1137	    RingHomSurjective (lastVarAlgHom r κ).toRingHom :=
1138	  ⟨lastVarAlgHom_surjective r⟩
1139	
1140	end LastVar

1142	/-! ### Finiteness transfer down one variable
1143	
1144	The keystone of the ambient subquotient induction: if a subquotient is finite over the free
1145	polynomial ring `MvPolynomial (Fin (r+1)) κ` and the last endomorphism annihilates it, then it is
1146	finite over `MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ`. The action factors through the `lastVarAlgHom` surjection. -/
1147	
1148	section Transfer
1149	
1150	variable {κ M : Type*} [Field κ] [AddCommGroup M] [Module κ M]
1151	
1152	/-- A `t`-stable submodule `P'` is closed under the action of any polynomial via `polyEndHom`. -/
1153	lemma polyEndHom_mem_of_stable {r : ℕ} (t : Fin r → Module.End κ M)
1154	    (hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)) {P' : Submodule κ M}
1155	    (hP' : ∀ i, P'.map (t i) ≤ P') (p : MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ) :
1156	    ∀ m ∈ P', (polyEndHom t hcomm p) m ∈ P' := by
1157	  induction p using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
1158	  | C a => intro m hm; rw [polyEndHom_C]; simpa using P'.smul_mem a hm
1159	  | add p q hp hq =>
1160	      intro m hm; rw [map_add, LinearMap.add_apply]; exact P'.add_mem (hp m hm) (hq m hm)
1161	  | mul_X p i hp =>
1162	      intro m hm; rw [map_mul, polyEndHom_X, Module.End.mul_apply]
1163	      exact hp _ (hP' i (Submodule.mem_map_of_mem hm))
1164	
1165	/-- **Mod-`P'` semilinearity heart.** For `m ∈ P`, evaluating a polynomial `s` via the full
1166	endomorphism family `t` agrees, modulo `P'`, with first projecting away the last variable
1167	(`lastVarAlgHom`) and evaluating via `t ∘ Fin.castSucc` — provided the last endomorphism
1168	`x = t (Fin.last r)` carries `P` into `P'` and `P, P'` are stable under every `t i`. This is the
1169	algebraic content of the finiteness transfer (`lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer`). -/
1170	lemma polyEndHom_lastVar_sub_mem {r : ℕ} (t : Fin (r + 1) → Module.End κ M)
1171	    (hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)) {P P' : Submodule κ M}
1172	    (hP : ∀ i, P.map (t i) ≤ P) (hP' : ∀ i, P'.map (t i) ≤ P')
1173	    (hannih : P.map (t (Fin.last r)) ≤ P')
1174	    (s : MvPolynomial (Fin (r + 1)) κ) :
1175	    ∀ m ∈ P, (polyEndHom t hcomm s) m
1176	      - (polyEndHom (fun i => t (Fin.castSucc i)) (fun i j => hcomm _ _)
1177	          (lastVarAlgHom r κ s)) m ∈ P' := by
1178	  induction s using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
1179	  | C a =>
1180	      intro m _
1181	      rw [lastVarAlgHom_C, polyEndHom_C, polyEndHom_C, sub_self]
1182	      exact P'.zero_mem
1183	  | add p q hp hq =>
1184	      intro m hm
1185	      rw [map_add, map_add, map_add, LinearMap.add_apply, LinearMap.add_apply]
1186	      have := P'.add_mem (hp m hm) (hq m hm)
1187	      convert this using 1
1188	      abel
1189	  | mul_X p j hp =>
1190	      intro m hm
1191	      rw [map_mul, polyEndHom_X, Module.End.mul_apply]
1192	      rcases Fin.eq_castSucc_or_eq_last j with ⟨i, rfl⟩ | rfl
1193	      · -- `j = castSucc i`: reduce to the IH at `t (castSucc i) m ∈ P`
1194	        rw [map_mul, lastVarAlgHom_X_castSucc, map_mul, polyEndHom_X, Module.End.mul_apply]
1195	        exact hp _ (hP _ (Submodule.mem_map_of_mem hm))
1196	      · -- `j = last`: the right term vanishes; the left lands in `P'` by annihilation
1197	        rw [map_mul, lastVarAlgHom_X_last, mul_zero, map_zero, LinearMap.zero_apply, sub_zero]
1198	        exact polyEndHom_mem_of_stable t hcomm hP' p _
1199	          (hannih (Submodule.mem_map_of_mem hm))
1200

1202	ambient submodules of `M`, stable under every `t i`) is finite over `MvPolynomial (Fin (r+1)) κ`

1205	`lastVarAlgHom` surjection; finiteness transfers by `Module.Finite.of_surjective`. -/

1330	/-! ### The ambient subquotient datum
1331	
1332	Bundles a homogeneous pair `N' ≤ N` of a fixed graded `κ`-module `M = ⨁ ℳ n` with `r`
1333	pairwise-commuting degree-raising endomorphisms preserving the pair, plus the finiteness of
1334	the represented subquotient `N/N'` over the free polynomial ring `MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ` (via
1335	`polySubmodule`, so the underlying carriers stay ambient submodules of `M`). This is the
1336	length-`r` carrier of the Stacks 00K1 ambient induction (`def:graded_subquotientHilb`). -/
1337	
1338	section Datum
1339	
1340	variable {κ M : Type*} [Field κ] [AddCommGroup M] [Module κ M]
1341	variable (ℳ : ℕ → Submodule κ M) [DirectSum.Decomposition ℳ]
1342	
1343	/-- A length-`r` **ambient subquotient datum** over the fixed graded module `M = ⨁ ℳ n`.
1344	Project-local: the carrier of the Stacks 00K1 ambient induction (`def:graded_subquotientHilb`).
1345	The finiteness field `hfin` records that the represented subquotient `N/N'` is a finite module
1346	over the free polynomial ring `MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ` acting through the `t i`; the carriers
1347	involved are the ambient `t`-stable submodules `polySubmodule … N`, `polySubmodule … N'`. -/
1348	structure SubquotientDatum (r : ℕ) where
1349	  /-- The upper homogeneous submodule. -/
1350	  N : Submodule κ M
1351	  /-- The lower homogeneous submodule. -/
1352	  N' : Submodule κ M
1353	  /-- `N'` is contained in `N`. -/
1354	  hle : N' ≤ N
1355	  /-- `N` is homogeneous. -/
1356	  hN : N.IsHomogeneous ℳ
1357	  /-- `N'` is homogeneous. -/
1358	  hN' : N'.IsHomogeneous ℳ
1359	  /-- The `r` degree-raising endomorphisms. -/
1360	  t : Fin r → Module.End κ M
1361	  /-- They pairwise commute. -/
1362	  hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)
1363	  /-- Each raises degree by one. -/
1364	  hraise : ∀ i, RaisesDegree ℳ (t i)
1365	  /-- Each preserves `N`. -/
1366	  hpresN : ∀ i, N.map (t i) ≤ N
1367	  /-- Each preserves `N'`. -/
1368	  hpresN' : ∀ i, N'.map (t i) ≤ N'
1369	  /-- The represented subquotient `N/N'` is finite over `MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ`. -/
1370	  hfin : letI := polyModule t hcomm
1371	    Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ)
1372	      (↥(polySubmodule t hcomm N hpresN) ⧸
1373	        (polySubmodule t hcomm N' hpresN').comap (polySubmodule t hcomm N hpresN).subtype)
1374	
1375	/-- The ambient Hilbert function `n ↦ dim_κ(N ⊓ ℳ n) − dim_κ(N' ⊓ ℳ n)` of a subquotient
1376	datum (`def:graded_subquotientHilb`). -/
1377	noncomputable def SubquotientDatum.hilb {r : ℕ} (D : SubquotientDatum ℳ r) : ℕ → ℚ :=
1378	  subquotientHilb ℳ D.N D.N'
1379	
1380	/-- The kernel pair's lower module `N ⊓ x⁻¹N'` is stable under every endomorphism of the family
1381	(needed for the finiteness transfer over the full polynomial ring). -/
1382	lemma ker_stable_full {r : ℕ} (D : SubquotientDatum ℳ (r + 1)) (i : Fin (r + 1)) :
1383	    (D.N ⊓ (D.N').comap (D.t (Fin.last r))).map (D.t i)
1384	      ≤ D.N ⊓ (D.N').comap (D.t (Fin.last r)) :=
1385	  le_trans (le_inf (Submodule.map_mono inf_le_left) (Submodule.map_mono inf_le_right))
1386	    (inf_le_inf (D.hpresN i) (comap_map_le_of_commute (D.hcomm (Fin.last r) i) (D.hpresN' i)))
1387	
1388	/-- The cokernel pair's upper module `N' ⊔ x·N` is stable under every endomorphism of the family. -/
1389	lemma coker_stable_full {r : ℕ} (D : SubquotientDatum ℳ (r + 1)) (i : Fin (r + 1)) :
1390	    (D.N' ⊔ D.N.map (D.t (Fin.last r))).map (D.t i)
1391	      ≤ D.N' ⊔ D.N.map (D.t (Fin.last r)) := by
1392	  rw [Submodule.map_sup]
1393	  exact sup_le_sup (D.hpresN' i)
1394	    (map_map_le_of_commute (D.hcomm (Fin.last r) i) (D.hpresN i))
1395	
1396	/-- **Kernel constructor.** From a length-`(r+1)` subquotient datum, the kernel subquotient
1397	`(N ⊓ x⁻¹N', N')` of multiplication by `x = t (last)`, as a length-`r` datum on `t ∘ castSucc`.
1398	All non-finiteness fields are the ambient kernel/cokernel calculus; the finiteness field is the
1399	keystone transfer `subquotient_finite_transfer` (`lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer`). -/
1400	noncomputable def SubquotientDatum.ker {r : ℕ} (D : SubquotientDatum ℳ (r + 1)) :
1401	    SubquotientDatum ℳ r where
1402	  N := D.N ⊓ (D.N').comap (D.t (Fin.last r))
1403	  N' := D.N'
1404	  hle := ker_le D.hle (D.hpresN' (Fin.last r))
1405	  hN := ker_isHomogeneous ℳ (D.hraise (Fin.last r)) D.hN D.hN'
1406	  hN' := D.hN'
1407	  t := fun i => D.t (Fin.castSucc i)
1408	  hcomm := fun i j => D.hcomm _ _
1409	  hraise := fun i => D.hraise _
1410	  hpresN := fun i => ker_stable_full ℳ D (Fin.castSucc i)
1411	  hpresN' := fun i => D.hpresN' _
1412	  hfin :=
1413	    subquotient_finite_transfer D.t D.hcomm (ker_stable_full ℳ D) D.hpresN'
1414	      ker_annihilate
1415	      (polyQuot_finite_of_le_numerator D.t D.hcomm (ker_stable_full ℳ D) D.hpresN D.hpresN'
1416	        inf_le_left D.hfin)
1417	
1418	/-- **Cokernel constructor.** From a length-`(r+1)` subquotient datum, the cokernel subquotient
1419	`(N, N' ⊔ x·N)` of multiplication by `x = t (last)`, as a length-`r` datum on `t ∘ castSucc`. -/

1440	since `MvPolynomial σ κ ≃ₐ[κ] κ`. Project-local: the base case of the Stacks 00K1 induction.
1441	Stated outside the `Datum` section as it needs no grading. -/
1442	lemma finiteDimensional_of_mvPolynomial_isEmpty_finite
1443	    {κ : Type*} [Field κ] {σ : Type*} [IsEmpty σ]
1444	    {Q : Type*} [AddCommGroup Q] [Module κ Q]
1445	    [Module (MvPolynomial σ κ) Q] [IsScalarTower κ (MvPolynomial σ κ) Q]
1446	    [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial σ κ) Q] : FiniteDimensional κ Q := by
1447	  haveI : Module.Finite κ (MvPolynomial σ κ) :=
1448	    Module.Finite.equiv (MvPolynomial.isEmptyAlgEquiv κ σ).symm.toLinearEquiv

1453	variable [∀ n, FiniteDimensional κ ↥(ℳ n)]

1455	/-- **Base case of the ambient subquotient induction.** A length-`0` subquotient datum has an

1457	`MvPolynomial (Fin 0) κ ≃ κ`, hence finite-dimensional over `κ`, so its degreewise pieces — an
1458	independent family inside the finite-dimensional quotient — vanish for large degree. -/
1459	lemma subquotient_base_eventuallyZero (D : SubquotientDatum ℳ 0) :
1460	    ∃ K, ∀ n, K < n → subquotientHilb ℳ D.N D.N' n = 0 := by
1461	  classical
1462	  letI := polyModule D.t D.hcomm
1463	  haveI hts : IsScalarTower κ (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) κ) M := polyModule_isScalarTower D.t D.hcomm
1464	  haveI := D.hfin
1465	  -- the subquotient `Q = N/N'` is finite-dimensional over `κ`
1466	  haveI hfd : FiniteDimensional κ (↥(polySubmodule D.t D.hcomm D.N D.hpresN) ⧸
1467	      (polySubmodule D.t D.hcomm D.N' D.hpresN').comap
1468	        (polySubmodule D.t D.hcomm D.N D.hpresN).subtype) :=
1469	    finiteDimensional_of_mvPolynomial_isEmpty_finite (σ := Fin 0)
1470	  set W : Submodule (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) κ) ↥(polySubmodule D.t D.hcomm D.N D.hpresN) :=
1471	    (polySubmodule D.t D.hcomm D.N' D.hpresN').comap
1472	      (polySubmodule D.t D.hcomm D.N D.hpresN).subtype with hW
1473	  set Q := ↥(polySubmodule D.t D.hcomm D.N D.hpresN) ⧸ W with hQ
1474	  -- the degreewise image map `ψ n : N ⊓ ℳ n →ₗ[κ] Q`, `m ↦ [m]`
1475	  set ψ : ∀ n, ↥(D.N ⊓ ℳ n) →ₗ[κ] Q := fun n =>
1476	    { toFun := fun m => Submodule.Quotient.mk ⟨(m : M), (Submodule.mem_inf.mp m.2).1⟩
1477	      map_add' := fun a b => by rw [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_add]; rfl
1478	      map_smul' := fun c a => by
1479	        simp only [RingHom.id_apply, SetLike.val_smul]
1480	        rw [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_smul]
1481	        rfl } with hψ
1482	  -- the ranges form an independent family in the finite-dimensional `Q`
1483	  -- (RESIDUAL LEAF — the only `sorry` in the QUOT keystone chain). For each `n`,
1484	  -- `Disjoint (range (ψ n)) (⨆ j ≠ n, range (ψ j))`: the degree-`n` projection `πₙ : M → M`,
